name: squat to overhead reach
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Lower your body down into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you come up from the squat, extend your arms overhead, reaching towards the ceiling.
Return to the starting position by lowering your arms and bending your knees to squat down again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. Lower your body down into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. As you come up from the squat, extend your arms overhead, reaching towards the ceiling. Return to the starting position by lowering your arms and bending your knees to squat down again. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Overhead positions can stress shoulder and elbow joints. Reduce ROM and load as needed.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Single-leg or balance-intensive variations increase fall risk. Use support or a seated alternative.
Avoid pushing into end-range positions. Prioritize midrange control and joint stability over depth.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
